method: 1-D Wasserstein-1 earth-mover distance between recent vs prior return-distribution windows (per-coin and cross-coin); de-risk overlay + standalone long-vol/directional.
libs_implemented: wasserstein1 (exact 1-D earth-mover via sorted-quantile L1, equal-size fast path + unequal-size trapezoid quantile integration); causal rolling W-distance series (per-coin & cross-coin pairwise-avg); causal z-score; long-variance replication payoff. All numpy/pandas only.
notes: 1-D Wasserstein-1 (earth-mover) regime detector, implemented from scratch (sorted-quantile L1). Tested as DE-RISK OVERLAY (primary) and standalone. PREMISE holds weakly: W-distance has positive rank-corr with next-5d realized vol (raw flavour ~ vol-clustering tautology; standardized/shape flavour weaker). OVERLAY headline=EWL: gating the base book when cross-coin W-z spikes gives OOS Sharpe +0.44 (gated) vs +0.19 (un-gated), maxDD -61.3% vs -68.3%; improves=YES; PSR_gated=0.721. STANDALONE best=direction OOS Sharpe=+0.89 PSR=0.878. n_variants_tried=90, DSR SR*=0.0617. ABLATION (key): vs a TRIVIAL realized-vol gate, W beats RV-gate = BTC:False EWL:True (W +0.44 vs RV +0.29). Most of the de-risk 'benefit' is the well-known fact that vol-spikes precede more vol; the Wasserstein math adds little over a one-line realized-vol gate, and after deflation it is fragile. close-to-close daily maxDD ignores intrabar liq/gap.
data_caveats: close-to-close daily futures closes; maxDD is close-to-close (no intrabar liq/gap) -> overstates calm; MATICUSDT dropped (delisted mid-sample, survivorship). Premise rank-corr vs fwd vol is diagnostic (uses future data) and NOT traded. Overlay base book is long-beta (directional), not market-neutral.
